parameters.name: vdd
parameters.override_behavior: sets the logic-high target for vout, edge_valid, and rejected

parameters.name: vss
parameters.override_behavior: sets the cleared and logic-low target for all public outputs

parameters.name: vth
parameters.override_behavior: sets the decision threshold for vin, rst, and enable

parameters.name: tick
parameters.override_behavior: sets the deterministic qualification and delay scheduling interval

parameters.name: delay_ticks
parameters.override_behavior: sets the additional number of scheduling ticks between qualification and output emission

parameters.name: min_width_ticks
parameters.override_behavior: sets the number of stable scheduling ticks required to qualify an input edge

parameters.name: tr
parameters.override_behavior: sets rise and fall smoothing for all public voltage outputs

modeling_constraints: AMS role: qualified edge-delay and deglitch block for mixed-signal clock, timing, and control paths.
Use deterministic voltage-domain threshold events and periodic scheduling state for qualification and delayed emission.
Use voltage contributions for vout, edge_valid, and rejected, with transition smoothing controlled by tr.
Preserve the positional six-port interface and all overrideable public parameters.
Do not use current contributions, transistor-level devices, random sources, external table files, AC/noise behavior, debug outputs, validation hooks, or simulator-specific side channels.

properties.observable_contract: When rst is above vth or enable is at or below vth, pending edge state is cancelled and vout, edge_valid, and rejected settle to vss no later than the next scheduling tick.
A rising or falling vin crossing through vth can qualify only when vin remains in the crossed-to logic state for min_width_ticks scheduling ticks while reset is inactive and the DUT is enabled.
After an input edge qualifies, vout changes to the corresponding vdd or vss target only after the additional delay_ticks scheduling interval and does not change early.
If vin reverses before a pending edge completes qualification, that edge does not update vout and rejected produces a bounded high pulse.
Each qualified delayed update of vout produces one bounded high pulse on edge_valid, while rejected remains reserved for cancelled narrow edges.
Qualified rising and falling input edges can respectively drive vout toward vdd and vss, and all public outputs use tr-smoothed voltage transitions.
Overriding tick, min_width_ticks, or delay_ticks changes the observable qualification or emission timing without changing module ports, output polarity, or reset/enable behavior.

title: Edge Delay Line with Deglitch Window
